title: Review: sieve-form MY-OSS round 2
notes:
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — wrong error kind for missing-require violations will mislead callers and be copied by contributors; Redirect has no caller guidance; efficiency issues are minor constant-factor only.
Confidence: 12% chance of missed bug. Least confident flags: (1) redirect and envelope extensions have no evaluator-level integration tests — the paths are implemented but untested; a typo or off-by-one in a future refactor would go undetected. (2) bare_address uses rfind('<') without respecting quoted strings in the pre-split input — a display name containing a literal '<' inside double quotes would cause bare_address to extract the wrong range; however split_addresses correctly guards the comma split, so only a perverse address like '"A <fake" <real@x.com>' in a single unsplit header field would hit this. Both are real gaps but neither is a correctness regression from the prior round. Bikeshedding: YES — running out of real complaints. Remaining items are test coverage gaps (not bugs) and a theoretical edge case in RFC 5322 quoted-string parsing that real mail does not exercise.
SEC0: CLEAN
